title: Family of man fatally shot by Syracuse officer seeks new trial in lawsuit
summary: The family of Gary Porter, fatally shot by Syracuse Police Officer Kelsey Francemone in 2016, has requested a new trial, arguing the jury verdict was contradictory and that Black jurors were improperly excluded. The jury found excessive force but also agreed Porter had a gun, awarding only $10,000 in damages. The case now hinges on whether Francemone qualifies for judicial immunity protection.

entity_refs.name: Chief U.S. District Judge Brenda Sannes

political_signal.description: High-profile civil rights litigation challenging police conduct and jury composition; qualified immunity doctrine under renewed examination; community equity concerns regarding jury selection procedures

processing_notes: Case spans nine-year litigation arc (2016 incident to 2025 post-trial motion)
Qualified immunity determination pending—outcome has significant policy implications for officer liability
Jury verdict exhibits apparent contradictions that defense counsel is leveraging for appeal; suggests juror confusion or compromise verdict
Jury composition challenge (Batson claim regarding Black juror dismissals) may trigger procedural review by CIO stakeholder departments if policy/training implications arise
